metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope/What section whether `idea.py` calls `create_core` (requiring `composer.py` to be added to scope with a new `status` parameter) or bypasses it to call `append_event` directly, and add the chosen path to the scope list.
findings.finding: `create_core` in `composer.py` (lines 72–171) does not accept a `status` parameter, yet the plan does not list `composer.py` in its Scope and does not explain how `idea.py` will inject `status='idea'` into the CREATE event data dict. Two paths are possible: (a) modify `create_core` to accept and forward `status` (requires adding `composer.py` to scope), or (b) have `idea.py` bypass `create_core` and call `append_event` directly. The plan is silent on this architectural decision, leaving a gap in how the CREATE event data will carry `status='idea'`.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` to the Scope list with a note: 'extend `create_core` to accept an optional `status` parameter (default `None`/`"open"`) forwarded into the event data dict, consumed only by `idea.py`; the general `create_cli` does not expose it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` to the scope list and specify whether `create_core` will gain a `status` kwarg (forwarded into the event data dict) or whether `idea.py` will call `append_event` directly, bypassing `create_core`.
findings.finding: `composer.py` (`create_core`) is an unenumerated edit target: `create_core` currently builds the CREATE event data dict with no `status` field, so `idea.py` cannot inject `status=idea` into the CREATE event by calling `create_core` as-is — it would either need `create_core` to be modified to accept+forward a `status` kwarg, or `idea.py` must bypass `create_core` and call `append_event` directly (duplicating all of `create_core`'s validation). Neither path is in scope, and `composer.py` is absent from the plan's scope list.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` to the scope list. Extend `create_core` with an optional `status: str | None = None` kwarg: if non-None, add `data['status'] = status` before calling `append_event`. Then call `create_core('epic', title, status='idea', description=description, repo_root=repo_root)` from `idea.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` and `src/rebar/_cli/help/idea.txt` to the scope list, and specify that `_dispatch()` will route `idea` in-process (e.g. via a new intercept block or by adding `idea` to `_WRITES_FULL`) and that `idea.txt` will be created with pinned help text.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` and `src/rebar/_cli/help/idea.txt` are unenumerated edit targets required to wire `rebar idea` into the CLI: `_dispatch()` in `__init__.py` needs a routing arm for `idea`, the subcommand must appear in `_WRITES_FULL` (or a new intercept block), and `known_subcommands()` is driven by `.txt` files in `_cli/help/` — without `idea.txt`, `main()` rejects `idea` as an unknown subcommand before `_dispatch` is ever called.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an intercept block in `_cli/__init__.py` `_dispatch()` (mirroring the `verify-commit-ticket` pattern): `if sub == 'idea': ensure_initialized(init_only=False); from rebar._commands import idea as _idea; return _idea.idea_cli(rest)`. Create `src/rebar/_cli/help/idea.txt` with the usage string.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the affected contract/facade test files (`tests/interfaces/contracts/test_help_overview_coverage.py`, `tests/interfaces/facades/test_cli_argparse.py`) to the scope list, or note explicitly that those tests cover `known_subcommands()` and will require updating when `idea` is added to the CLI dispatch.
findings.finding: The consumer contract tests `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_help_overview_coverage.py` and `tests/interfaces/facades/test_cli_argparse.py` enumerate `known_subcommands()` and the CLI dispatch sets (`_WRITES_FULL`, etc.); adding `idea` to the CLI without updating these tests will cause existing test failures. Neither test file is mentioned in the plan's scope.

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the validation/fallback rule in `process_create` for a CREATE event `status` value that is non-null but not a recognized status (e.g., clamp to `open`, raise a fsck error, or accept-verbatim with a rationale) so the decision rule is co-located with the schema change.
findings.finding: `process_create` is specified to 'honor optional status, default to open when absent' but no validation rule is defined for an invalid/unrecognized status value appearing on a CREATE event — the decision rule (reject? clamp to open? persist verbatim?) is absent from the plan, leaving a structural gap in the processing protocol co-located with the schema change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the 'idea is the sole non-`open` genesis-status producer; no general `create --status` flag' constraint and its race-condition rationale in a durable in-repo artifact (e.g., an expanded note in `docs/event-schema.md`, a CLAUDE.md entry, or a short ADR) so the invariant survives beyond the story text.
findings.finding: The design decision that `idea` is the *sole* non-`open` genesis-status producer (i.e., no general `create --status` flag) and the race-condition rationale for atomic creation are captured only in the ephemeral story text, not in any durable design document (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or an expanded section of docs/event-schema.md). A future contributor modifying `process_create` has no in-repo signal that this constraint is intentional.
findings.location: ## What — 'No general create --status flag is added' / docs/event-schema.md update (AC line 11)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Design constraints' subsection to `docs/event-schema.md` (or a new `docs/adr/NNN-atomic-idea-creation.md`) stating: (1) a CREATE event MAY carry `status`; (2) the only sanctioned caller that sets a non-`open` genesis status is the `idea` command; (3) rationale is race-condition avoidance (a parallel agent could claim an `open` ticket before a STATUS transition fires).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC10's implementation path: either add `composer.py` to scope and note where the warning suppression is coded, OR explicitly state that `idea.py` calls `create_core` directly (bypassing `create_cli`) so the warning never fires by design.
findings.finding: The file-impact warning suppression for `idea`-created epics is listed as a plan deliverable (AC10) and mentioned in the What section, but `composer.py` — where the warning lives — is not in scope. The warning is emitted in `create_cli` (composer.py lines 263-276), conditioned on `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES`. If `idea.py` routes through `create_cli` or `create_core`, the warning fires for `epic` (not in `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES`); if `idea.py` bypasses both, there is no warning to suppress. Either way, `composer.py` requires modification.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the file_impact warning suppression mechanism: state whether `idea.py` avoids `create_cli` entirely (making suppression automatic), or whether `composer.py`'s `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` / warning condition must be modified — and if the latter, specify the exact condition to avoid suppressing the warning for non-idea epic creates.
findings.finding: The AC 'The create-time file_impact stderr warning does not fire for a ticket created by `rebar idea`' specifies an outcome but not the mechanism: `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES = ("bug", "session_log")` in `composer.py` is the sole suppression gate, and adding `epic` to it would suppress the warning for ALL epic creates (not just idea-created ones). If `idea.py` bypasses `create_cli` entirely, no change is needed — but this decision rule is never stated.
findings.location: AC: 'The create-time file_impact stderr warning does not fire for a ticket created by `rebar idea`' / `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` lines 41, 268
findings.suggested_fix: State explicitly: 'Because `idea.py` calls `create_core` directly (not `create_cli`) and performs its own output formatting, the warning code in `create_cli` is never reached and no change to `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` is needed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit note acknowledging that `process_create` previously ignored `status` in CREATE events, confirm that no existing event logs contain this field (or provide a migration/rollback path if they might), and state the rollback approach if the change needs to be reverted.
findings.finding: The change to `process_create` to honor a `status` field in CREATE event data means that any pre-existing stored CREATE events which coincidentally contain a `status` key (previously silently ignored) would now have their ticket status set from that field on replay/rehydration. The plan does not acknowledge this behavioral change to existing event logs, nor provide a rollback or migration note.
findings.location: ## What — '`process_create` honors an optional `status` on the CREATE event, defaulting to `open` when absent'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Compatibility' sub-section under 'What' stating: 'Existing CREATE events without a `status` field are unaffected (default `open` preserved). We have audited stored event logs and confirmed no existing CREATE event carries a `status` key; if one were found, the field would be ignored under a feature-flag rollback.' Alternatively, add a one-time migration assertion/check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge (or rule out) the replay-compatibility implication: either assert that no existing event logs contain CREATE events with a `status` field, or note that this is an intentional behavior upgrade and document it in `docs/event-schema.md`.
findings.finding: `process_create` currently silently ignores any `status` field present in a CREATE event. After this change it will honor it, meaning replay of any historical CREATE event that happens to carry a `status` field will now produce a different ticket state than it did before. The plan does not acknowledge this potential replay-correctness change.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line note in the plan (and in `docs/event-schema.md`) stating that CREATE events with a `status` field were previously ignored and will now be honored starting from this change, so teams with manually-authored event logs should audit for unexpected `status` fields.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the 'What' section (under `process_create` / `idea.py`) explicitly stating that `idea.py` places `status='idea'` directly in the CREATE event's `data` dict (e.g. by constructing the payload itself rather than delegating to `create_core`, which has no `status` parameter), so that the single-event atomicity guarantee is achieved at the event-composition layer, not via a subsequent STATUS event.
findings.finding: The plan does not make explicit that `idea.py` will compose the CREATE event's `data` dict directly (including `status='idea'`) rather than calling `create_core` (which accepts no `status` parameter) and then emitting a STATUS event afterward. An implementer reading the scope list ('src/rebar/_commands/idea.py — new atomic CLI command') without the rationale section might reach for the two-step path, which the plan's own 'Why' section rules out. The plan states the desired outcome (single CREATE event) in ACs but does not state the positive mechanism by which `idea.py` achieves it — specifically, that `idea.py` must pass `status='idea'` inside the CREATE event's data payload (not via a follow-on STATUS event) because `create_core` has no `status` param.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope — the `idea.py` entry and `process_create` extension description
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' bullet for `process_create`, add: 'The `idea` command achieves this by including `"status": "idea"` in the CREATE event's `data` dict at composition time (not via a follow-on STATUS event); `idea.py` builds this data payload directly rather than routing through the general `create_core`, which has no `status` parameter.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test file path(s) (e.g. `tests/test_idea_command.py`) to the Scope section and ensure at least one AC maps each surface (CLI, MCP, library) to a concrete test that inspects the event log for a single CREATE event with no intervening STATUS event.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists only source and documentation files — no test files are named — yet multiple ACs require test-level verification (e.g., 'test inspects the event log' for the single-CREATE guarantee, the warning-suppression check, and the per-surface behavioral checks). The union of described steps does not reach the stated end-state because the test artifacts that would prove the ACs are never scheduled.
findings.location: Scope section; AC 'The ticket created by `rebar idea` is produced by a single CREATE event with no intervening STATUS event (test inspects the event log).'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `tests/test_idea_command.py` (or equivalent). Map ACs to specific test functions: e.g., `test_idea_single_create_event`, `test_idea_mcp_single_create_event`, `test_idea_library_single_create_event`, `test_idea_warning_suppressed`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test file paths (e.g., `tests/test_idea_command.py` and/or relevant integration test file) to the Scope section to give each test-dependent AC a named deliverable.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists only source and documentation files; no test file is named, yet multiple ACs require test implementations (e.g., AC5: 'test inspects the event log', AC13: 'smoke test demonstrates the user-facing flow', plus implied coverage for MCP/library/warning ACs). This leaves tests as an uncovered deliverable with no scope entry.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to Scope such as: `tests/test_idea_command.py` (or the project's existing test layout equivalent) covering ACs 1-2 (process_create), 3-6 (CLI), 7 (MCP), 8 (library), 9 (no --status flag), 10 (warning suppression), and 13 (smoke test if applicable).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add ACs (or a validation section) specifying what each surface does when the title is empty/whitespace: expected error message for CLI, error response shape for MCP tool, and exception type for the library.
findings.finding: No AC or plan section specifies error behavior when an empty or whitespace-only title is supplied to `rebar idea`, `create_idea`, or `rebar.idea(...)`. All three surfaces are user-facing, but only the happy path (valid title string) is covered.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC for the invalid/empty-title failure path on `rebar idea` (e.g., 'Running `rebar idea ""` exits non-zero with a human-readable error and creates no ticket').
findings.finding: The new user-facing `rebar idea` command has no AC covering invalid or empty title input. A blank or whitespace-only title is a natural boundary condition for a command whose sole required argument is a free-text title, and the plan introduces no validation AC for this path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — `rebar idea` ACs (lines: `rebar idea "<title>"` creates a ticket of type epic, etc.)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: '`rebar idea` with an empty or whitespace-only title exits with a non-zero status and emits a descriptive error message; no ticket is created (verified by checking the event log is unchanged).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
